steps.title: Identify what we are checking
steps.content: A point satisfies this system if it makes both inequalities true. We need to verify both $y \leq 3x + 1$ AND $x - y > 1$ for our answer.

steps.title: Backsolve through the choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice A $(-2, -1)$:
Is $-1 \leq 3(-2) + 1 = -5$? No. Fails.

Testing choice B $(-1, 3)$:
Is $3 \leq 3(-1) + 1 = -2$? No. Fails.

Testing choice C $(1, 5)$:
Is $5 \leq 3(1) + 1 = 4$? No. Fails.

Testing choice D $(2, -1)$:
Inequality 1: Is $-1 \leq 3(2) + 1 = 7$? Yes. ✓
Inequality 2: Is $2 - (-1) = 3 > 1$? Yes. ✓
Both inequalities satisfied. That matches choice D — D is correct.
